what: Random Planted Forest: A Directly Interpretable Tree Ensemble
title: Random Planted Forest: A Directly Interpretable Tree Ensemble
text: randomPlantedForest implements “Random Planted Forest”, directly interpretable tree ensemble (arxiv).

title: Random Planted Forest: A Directly Interpretable Tree Ensemble
text: can install development version randomPlantedForest GitHub r-universe

title: Random Planted Forest: A Directly Interpretable Tree Ensemble
text: Model fitting uses familiar interface: Prediction components can accessed via predict_components, including intercept, main effects, interactions specified degree. returned object also contains original data x, required visualization. glex package can used well: glex(rpfit) yields result. Various visualization options available via glex, e.g. main second-order interaction effects:  See Bikesharing decomposition article examples.
code: library(randomPlantedForest)  mtcars$cyl <- factor(mtcars$cyl) rpfit <- rpf(mpg ~ cyl + wt + hp, data = mtcars, ntrees = 25, max_interaction = 2) rpfit #> -- Regression Random Planted Forest -- #>  #> Formula: mpg ~ cyl + wt + hp  #> Fit using 3 predictors and 2-degree interactions. #> Forest is _not_ purified! #>  #> Called with parameters: #>  #>             loss: L2 #>           ntrees: 25 #>  max_interaction: 2 #>           splits: 30 #>        split_try: 10 #>            t_try: 0.4 #>            delta: 0 #>          epsilon: 0.1 #>    deterministic: FALSE #>         nthreads: 1 #>           purify: FALSE #>               cv: FALSE  predict(rpfit, new_data = mtcars) |>   cbind(mpg = mtcars$mpg) |>   head() #>      .pred  mpg #> 1 20.96451 21.0 #> 2 21.06539 21.0 #> 3 23.24140 22.8 #> 4 21.17155 21.4 #> 5 18.22740 18.7 #> 6 18.76736 18.1 components <- predict_components(rpfit, new_data = mtcars)   str(components) #> List of 3 #>  $ m        :Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame':  32 obs. of  6 variables: #>   ..$ cyl   : num [1:32] 0.224 0.224 0.873 0.224 -0.803 ... #>   ..$ wt    : num [1:32] 0.177 -0.102 1.229 -0.154 -0.863 ... #>   ..$ hp    : num [1:32] 1.253 1.253 1.559 1.253 -0.586 ... #>   ..$ cyl:wt: num [1:32] -0.0516 0.0326 -0.0978 0.0279 0.1207 ... #>   ..$ cyl:hp: num [1:32] -0.0549 -0.0549 0.1872 -0.0549 0.3816 ... #>   ..$ hp:wt : num [1:32] -0.5891 -0.2933 -0.5148 -0.1305 -0.0282 ... #>   ..- attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<externalptr>  #>  $ intercept: num 20 #>  $ x        :Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame':  32 obs. of  3 variables: #>   ..$ cyl: Factor w/ 3 levels "4","6","8": 2 2 1 2 3 2 3 1 1 2 ... #>   ..$ wt : num [1:32] 2.62 2.88 2.32 3.21 3.44 ... #>   ..$ hp : num [1:32] 110 110 93 110 175 105 245 62 95 123 ... #>   ..- attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<externalptr>  #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr [1:3] "glex" "rpf_components" "list" # install glex if not available: if (!requireNamespace("glex")) remotes::install_github("PlantedML/glex") #> Loading required namespace: glex library(glex) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) # For plot arrangement  p1 <- autoplot(components, "wt") p2 <- autoplot(components, "hp") p3 <- autoplot(components, "cyl") p4 <- autoplot(components, c("wt", "hp"))  (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4) +   plot_annotation(     title = "Selected effects for mtcars",     caption = "(It's a tiny dataset but it has to fit in a README, okay?)"   )

title: Random Planted Forest Predictions — predict.rpf
text: object fit object class rpf. new_data Data new observations predict. type "numeric" regression outcomes, "class" class predictions "prob" probability predictions. classification loss = "L1" "L2", "numeric" yields raw predictions guaranteed valid probabilities [0, 1]. type = "prob", truncated ensure property. loss "logit" "exponential", type = "link" alias type = "numeric", case raw predictions additional interpretation similar linear predictor glm. ... Unused.

title: Random Planted Forest Predictions — predict.rpf
text: regression: tbl column .pred number rows new_data. classification: tbl one column level y containing class probabilities type = "prob". type = "class", one column .pred class predictions returned. type = "numeric" "link", one column .pred raw predictions.

title: Random Planted Forest Predictions — predict.rpf
code: # Regression with L2 loss rpfit <- rpf(y = mtcars$mpg, x = mtcars[, c("cyl", "wt")]) predict(rpfit, mtcars[, c("cyl", "wt")]) #> # A tibble: 32 × 1 #>    .pred #>    <dbl> #>  1  20.7 #>  2  20.3 #>  3  25.2 #>  4  21.2 #>  5  17.1 #>  6  18.6 #>  7  15.1 #>  8  24.1 #>  9  23.0 #> 10  19.4 #> # ℹ 22 more rows

what: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
text: Prediction components functional decomposition model prediction. sum components equals overall predicted value observation.

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
code: predict_components(object, new_data, max_interaction = NULL, predictors = NULL)

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
text: object fit object class rpf. new_data Data new observations predict. max_interaction integer NULL: Maximum degree interactions consider. Default use max_interaction parameter rpf object. Must 1 (main effects ) max_interaction rpf object. predictors character NULL: Vector one column names predictor variables new_data extract components . NULL, variables interactions returned.

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
text: list elements: m (data.table): Components main effect interaction term, representing functional decomposition prediction. components together intercept sum prediction. multiclass classification, number output columns multiplied number levels outcome. intercept (numeric(1)): Expected value prediction. x (data.table): Copy new_data containing predictors selected predictors. target_levels (character): multiclass classification : Vector target levels can used disassemble m, names include term target level.

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
text: Extracts possible components max_interaction degrees, value set calling rpf(). intercept always included. Optionally predictors can specified include components including given variables. max_interaction greater length(predictors), max_interaction lowered accordingly.

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
text: Depending number predictors max_interaction, number components increase drastically sum(choose(ncol(new_data), seq_len(max_interaction))).

title: Extract predicted components from a Random Planted Forest — predict_components
Preprocess predictors for prediction — preprocess_predictors_predict
text: Convert logical character columns appropriate types, re-order factor levels match ordering learned fitting (stored object$factor_levels), re-encode factor columns integers, return numeric matrix suitable underlying C++ prediction routines.

title: Preprocess predictors for prediction — preprocess_predictors_predict
text: object object class rpf returned rpf(). predictors data frame matrix predictor values preprocess.

title: Preprocess predictors for prediction — preprocess_predictors_predict
text: numeric matrix number rows predictors.

title: Preprocess predictors for prediction — preprocess_predictors_predict

title: Preprocess predictors for prediction — preprocess_predictors_predict
code: rpfit <- rpf(x = mtcars[, c("cyl", "wt")], y = mtcars$mpg) processed <- hardhat::forge(mtcars[, c("cyl", "wt")], rpfit$blueprint) X <- preprocess_predictors_predict(rpfit, processed$predictors) dim(X) #> [1] 32  2

title: Purify a Random Planted Forest — purify
code: purify(x, ...)  # Default S3 method purify(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'rpf' purify(x, ..., maxp_interaction = NULL, mode = 2L, nthreads = NULL)  is_purified(x)

title: Purify a Random Planted Forest — purify
text: x object class rpf. ... (Unused) maxp_interaction integer NULL: compute/store purified components interaction order. Higher-order purified trees zeroed (computed) still implicitly influence lower orders purification. NULL, purify orders (default behavior). mode integer(1): Purification algorithm mode. 1 = legacy grid path used fit$fit$purify(); 2 = fast exact KD-tree based path. Defaults 2. nthreads integer NULL: number threads use. NULL, defaults min object's configured nthreads available threads.

title: Purify a Random Planted Forest — purify
text: Invisibly: rpf object.

title: Purify a Random Planted Forest — purify
text: Unless rpf() called purify = TRUE, forest purified fit ensure components extracted predict_components() valid. predict_components() automatically purify forest is_purified() reports FALSE.

title: Purify a Random Planted Forest — purify
code: rpfit <- rpf(mpg ~., data = mtcars, max_interaction = 2, ntrees = 10) purify(rpfit) #> -- Regression Random Planted Forest -- #>  #> Formula: mpg ~ .  #> Fit using 10 predictors and 2-degree interactions. #> Forest is purified! #>  #> Called with parameters: #>  #>              loss: L2 #>            ntrees: 10 #>   max_interaction: 2 #>            splits: 30 #>         split_try: 10 #>             t_try: 0.4 #>  split_decay_rate: 0.1 #>    max_candidates: 50 #>     delete_leaves: TRUE #>   split_structure: leaves #>             delta: 0 #>           epsilon: 0.1 #>     deterministic: FALSE #>          nthreads: 1 #>            purify: FALSE #>                cv: FALSE

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
text: Random Planted Forest

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
code: rpf(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' rpf(   x,   y,   max_interaction = 1,   ntrees = 50,   splits = 30,   split_try = 10,   t_try = 0.4,   split_decay_rate = 0.1,   max_candidates = 50,   delete_leaves = TRUE,   deterministic = FALSE,   nthreads = 1,   purify = FALSE,   cv = FALSE,   loss = "L2",   delta = 0,   epsilon = 0.1,   split_structure = "leaves",   export_forest = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rpf(   x,   y,   max_interaction = 1,   ntrees = 50,   splits = 30,   split_try = 10,   t_try = 0.4,   split_decay_rate = 0.1,   max_candidates = 50,   delete_leaves = TRUE,   deterministic = FALSE,   nthreads = 1,   purify = FALSE,   cv = FALSE,   loss = "L2",   delta = 0,   epsilon = 0.1,   split_structure = "leaves",   export_forest = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'formula' rpf(   formula,   data,   max_interaction = 1,   ntrees = 50,   splits = 30,   split_try = 10,   t_try = 0.4,   split_decay_rate = 0.1,   max_candidates = 50,   delete_leaves = TRUE,   deterministic = FALSE,   nthreads = 1,   purify = FALSE,   cv = FALSE,   loss = "L2",   delta = 0,   epsilon = 0.1,   split_structure = "leaves",   export_forest = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'recipe' rpf(   x,   data,   max_interaction = 1,   ntrees = 50,   splits = 30,   split_try = 10,   t_try = 0.4,   split_decay_rate = 0.1,   max_candidates = 50,   delete_leaves = TRUE,   deterministic = FALSE,   nthreads = 1,   purify = FALSE,   cv = FALSE,   loss = "L2",   delta = 0,   epsilon = 0.1,   split_structure = "leaves",   export_forest = FALSE,   ... )

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
text: x, data Feature matrix, data.frame, recipe. ... (Unused). y Target vector use x. class y (either numeric factor) determines regression classification performed. max_interaction [1]: Maximum level interaction determining maximum number split dimensions tree. default 1 corresponds main effects . 0, number fo columns x used, .e. 10 predictors, equivalent setting max_interaction = 10. ntrees [50]: Number trees generated per family. splits [30]: Number splits performed tree family. split_try [10]: Number split points considered choosing split candidate. t_try [0.4]: value (0,1] specifying proportion viable split-candidates round. split_decay_rate [0.1]: Exponential decay factor aging split-candidates. Possible splits initiated age=0. Whenever possible split becomes split_candidate (.e. drawn drawing max(max_candidates , t_try * possible options ) times) ages +1. age single split-candidate minimal loss reset zero. Split_candidates sampled Possible_splits weight exp(-split_decay_rate_ * age).  high split_decay_rate means faster aging. split_decay_rate=0 results aging uniform sampling. max_candidates [50]: Maximum number split-candidates sampled per iteration. Number split_candidates round given  max(max_candidates , t_try * possible options). delete_leaves [TRUE]: Whether delete parent leaf splitting along existing dimension. deterministic [FALSE]: Choose whether approach deterministic random. nthreads [1L]: Number threads used computation, defaulting serial execution. purify [FALSE]: Whether forest purified. Set TRUE enable components extract predict_components() valid. Can achieved fitting purify(). cv [FALSE]: Determines cross validation performed. loss ["L2"]: regression, "L2" supported. classification, "L1", "logit" "exponential" also available. "exponential" yields similar results "logit" significantly faster. delta [0]: used loss "logit" "exponential". Proportion class membership truncated smaller 1-delta calculating loss determine optimal split. epsilon [0.1]: used loss = "logit" "exponential". Proportion class membership truncated smaller 1-epsilon calculating fit leaf. split_structure ["leaves"]: Defines structure possible split choose split_candidates. Can one "leaves", "hist", "cur_trees_1", "cur_trees_2", "res_trees". details given . export_forest [FALSE]: Whether store flattened forest returned object $forest. FALSE, $forest NULL, reducing memory use returned object. formula Formula specification, e.g. y ~ x1 + x2.

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
text: Object class "rpf" model object contained $fit.

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
text: number splits main tuning parameter affecting accuracy predictions.

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
text: split_structure argument controls split candidates constructed sampled. round, t_try fraction (capped max_candidates) drawn pool possible splits weights exp(-split_decay_rate * age). leaves Split candidates (leaf, split-dimension) pairs. sampled candidate, split_try thresholds drawn uniformly valid range within leaf evaluated choose best split. cur_trees_1 Split candidates (current-tree, split-dimension) pairs. sampled candidate, perform split_try evaluations. evaluation samples leaf set valid current trees (probability proportional number available thresholds) uniformly samples single threshold within leaf. cur_trees_2 Split candidates (current-tree, split-dimension) pairs. sampled candidate, iterate every valid leaf. Within leaf, sample split_try thresholds uniformly evaluate . res_trees Split candidates resulting trees. sampled candidate, run split_try evaluations sampling (split-dimension, leaf) pair valid pairs (probability proportional number available thresholds), uniformly sampling one threshold within pair.

title: Random Planted Forest — rpf
code: # Regression with x and y rpfit <- rpf(x = mtcars[, c("cyl", "wt")], y = mtcars$mpg)  # Regression with formula rpfit <- rpf(mpg ~ cyl + wt, data = mtcars)

title: randomPlantedForest 0.3.0
text: split_structure = "leaves": Defines split candidate candidates drawn. One "leaves" (default), "hist", "cur_trees_1", "cur_trees_2", "res_trees"; see ?rpf details. max_candidates = 50: Maximum number split candidates sampled per iteration. split_decay_rate = 0.1: Exponential aging repeatedly drawn unchosen split candidates. split_decay_rate = 0 corresponds aging uniform sampling. delete_leaves = TRUE: Whether parent leaf deleted splitting along existing dimension. Fitting results change: new candidate-sampling defaults reworked internal RNG mean fits reproducible previous versions, even seed. Install older commit exact reproduction previous results required. Seeded fits now reproducible regardless nthreads: per-tree seeds drawn R’s RNG, set.seed() gives identical forests serial multithreaded fits. Substantial speedups fitting (cached per-leaf orderings, prefix sums) reduced memory use (training-buffers released tree family built). mode = 2: Purification algorithm; 2 new fast exact method, 1 legacy grid-based path. nthreads = NULL: Purification now multithreaded, defaulting fit’s nthreads setting. maxp_interaction = NULL: Optionally compute purified components interaction order. New rpf() argument export_forest = FALSE: flattened forest longer stored fitted object default, rpf_object$forest NULL unless export_forest = TRUE. reduces object size; predict(), purify(), predict_components() unaffected. preprocess_predictors_predict() now exported. Fixed memory bug legacy purification path grid sized one element large, causing --bounds reads (crashes Windows, silently wrong purification results elsewhere). Fixed crash Windows fitting nthreads > 1, caused thread_local buffer non-trivial destructor destroyed thread exit.

title: randomPlantedForest 0.3.0
text: Internals src/ refactored modular sub-files (#53) rpf() now errors regression target combined loss "L2". Allow features type logical, now converted via .integer. parallel = TRUE|FALSE argument rpf() substituted nthreads = 1L argument, allowing flexible parallelization. previous behavior allowed either parallelization using n-1 n available cores. new implementation reasonably robust default behavior remains serial execution. Remove SystemRequirements field DESCRIPTION: Now default C++ version C++17 minor change internal use random numbers, randomPlantedForest now compatible C++11 C++23. Add remainder term predict_components output case max_interaction supplied smaller max_interaction rpf fit. case, m values don’t sum global predictions, add remainder allow reconstruction property.

title: randomPlantedForest 0.2.1
text: Add glex class output predict_components(), extended functionality available glex. Add target_levels vector output predict_components() aid multiclass handling. Keeping track levels somewhat awkward since column names $m need identifiable regarding target level.

title: randomPlantedForest 0.2.0
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
